_note: Original, citation-anchored dream-symbol lexicon (NOT copied passages). Grounded in the depth-psychology tradition. sourceRefs point at standalone/interpretation-sources.json IDs so every interpretation can display its source. Meanings favor the archetypal/Jungian reading, with Freudian association noted where relevant. Edit and extend freely.

symbols.meaning: the unconscious and the flow of feeling. Calm water mirrors emotional peace; turbulent, murky, or rising water points to feelings that have not yet been faced.
the vast, collective unconscious — the depth and mystery of the psyche far below waking awareness. Being on it, in it, or under it shows your relationship to that depth.
a loss of control or footing — anxiety about a situation where you feel unsupported, overextended, or afraid to let go. It often surfaces at the edge of sleep and at the edge of a life change.
liberation and the wish to rise above a situation — freedom, perspective, or transcendence. Its shadow is escapism: leaving the ground of ordinary life rather than dealing with it.
anxiety about power, appearance, or being able to speak and be understood — often tied to vitality, aging, or a transition where you fear losing your footing or your voice.
avoidance — something in the psyche you are refusing to turn and face. What pursues you is usually not an enemy but a disowned part of yourself asking to be met.
transformation, not literal loss — the ending of a phase, role, or old self so something new can be born. The psyche clears the ground for renewal.
instinctual life-force and transformation — the serpent sheds its skin, so it carries healing and renewal, but also hidden danger and the raw, untamed energy of the unconscious.
the self and its many rooms. Upper floors suggest mind and memory; the basement or cellar is the unconscious; unknown rooms are undiscovered parts of who you are.
vulnerability and exposure — the fear of being seen as you truly are, or, more hopefully, the shedding of pretense and a return to authenticity.
a new beginning and vulnerable potential — a fresh part of the self, a project, or a possibility that is young and needs protecting.
transformation through intensity — passion, anger, or purification. Fire destroys, but it also clears and renews; what is burning shows what is being changed.
the direction and control of your life. Notice who is driving, whether the brakes work, and where you are headed — it mirrors how much agency you feel over your path.
transition — a crossing between two states, phases, or ways of being. What waits on the far side is the change you are moving toward.
a threshold and an opportunity — a new possibility or an unknown to step through. A locked door names something withheld or not yet ready.
self-reflection and identity — how you see yourself, and whether that image matches who you feel you are. A distorted reflection points to a self-image in question.
the Jungian shadow — the disowned, unacknowledged parts of yourself. Threatening in the dark, it becomes an ally once it is recognized and integrated rather than fled.
self-worth and personal resources — your sense of value and vitality. Finding it suggests recognized worth; losing it, a fear of scarcity or of not being enough.
the unknown reaches of the unconscious — a place of being lost, tested, or transformed. To enter the woods is to leave the map behind for a while.
anxiety about missed opportunity, timing, or preparation — a fear of not being ready, or that a chance is slipping past before you can reach it.
a change of level in the psyche — climbing suggests effort toward higher awareness or status; descending, a return to the unconscious, the body, or the past.
a fear of being judged or found wanting — feeling tested by life and unsure you have prepared enough, even when you are more ready than you believe.
a blocked connection — a longing to reach someone or some part of yourself, and the frustration of the message not getting through.
life-force and what is essential — vitality, sacrifice, or a wound that asks for attention; how much and whose blood colors the meaning.
the weaver and the trap — creative, patient, feminine power, but also the sense of being caught in a web, whether one you spun or one spun around you.
spirit, thought, and the soul that rises above — freedom, a message, or the part of you able to take the higher view.
loyalty and faithful instinct — a friendly dog is an ally within you; a threatening one, an instinct you fear or have not befriended.
independence and intuition — the mysterious, self-possessed side of instinct that moves by its own law and cannot be commanded.
confinement — a role, pattern, or situation you feel unable to leave, surfacing so it can finally be seen and questioned.
an inner union — the joining of two parts of yourself, or your real feelings about commitment, partnership, and belonging.
unfinished emotional business, or a quality that person carried that you are being asked to reclaim in yourself — rarely literally about them.
being overwhelmed by emotion or the unconscious — feelings rising faster than you can process, asking you to come up for air and reach for support.
emotional upheaval and released tension — a charge built up in the psyche breaking loose, frightening but ultimately clearing the air.
your life direction and the choice before you — two real ways forward, asking which one you are ready to commit to.
something unresolved from the past still moving through the present — a memory, a grief, or a part of the self not yet laid to rest.
access and solution — the means to open something closed, or a piece of knowledge or self-understanding you are just now coming into.
